title: helictochloa pratensis subsp. gonzaloi
intro: Helictochloa pratensis subsp. gonzaloi, also known as Gonzalo's meadow-grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Helictochloa pratensis subsp. gonzaloi include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Helical preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
